message:
refactor(tracing,bd-urscl): collapse 3-tier branches via local emit_tier! macro

Two hot-path tracing call sites repeated the same 6-8 structured
fields across three severity-tier branches that differed only in
the tracing::{warn,info,debug}! macro choice and one message:

1. src/indexer/refresh_ledger.rs::emit_tracing_summary — three
   branches (WARN significant slowdown / INFO notable improvement /
   DEBUG cross-run comparison) each emitted identical 6-field
   payload (aggregate_duration_delta_pct, aggregate_throughput_delta_pct,
   aggregate_duration, aggregate_throughput, dominant_phase_shift,
   phase_count).

2. src/indexer/lexical_generation.rs::record_inventory — three
   branches (DEBUG reclaimable / WARN pending operator inspection /
   INFO retained by policy) each emitted identical 8-field payload
   (generation_id, disposition, reason, reclaimable_bytes,
   retained_bytes, artifact_bytes, shard_count, inspection_required).

Risk pre-fix: a field added to one branch but forgotten in the
other two would silently ship to operator logs in only one severity
tier, leaving the other two tiers' events with stale shape.

Refactor: at each site, declare a local `macro_rules! emit_tier`
that inlines the shared payload at three call sites, parameterized
by (macro_ident, message_literal). No runtime cost — same code
generation as before. Per-tier difference reduced to a single line.
~80 LOC of pattern-repetition removed across both sites without any
new abstractions (tracing::$macro inside a local macro is the
idiomatic way to share a structured-field payload across multiple
severity tiers in tracing).

Validation: rch test pass for both severity gates —
- evidence_comparison_emit_tracing_summary_uses_correct_severity_tier
- record_inventory_emits_correct_severity_for_every_disposition_variant
2/2 pass, 23s, exit=0.

Closes coding_agent_session_search-urscl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
